summary: Corrected the token-retrieval instructions across the CLI: replaced v1.0's inaccurate "pair the CLI from the Snipd mobile app" language with the verified browser sign-in flow, and filled the dead-end `auth setup` placeholder with the concrete 4-step protocol.
reason: v1.0 described the Snipd account Bearer token as "obtained by pairing the CLI from the Snipd mobile app." The token session (2026-07-14) verified three independent ways — Snipd's open-source Obsidian plugin source (github.com/snipd-app/snipd-obsidian), a live view of the sign-in page, and the original probe artifacts in discovery/probes/private/ — that the token is minted by a BROWSER sign-in to the user's own Snipd account and requires neither the mobile app, Obsidian, nor the Snipd Obsidian plugin. The sign-in page is titled "Obsidian integration" only because that is Snipd's internal name for this export API; it is a label, not a dependency. This patch records the hand-edits to two hand-authored source files (internal/cli/pull.go no-token runtime hint; internal/snipd/client.go 401/403 error — both already within the base 'snipd-source-and-retrieval-commands' hand-authored scope, header "NOT generator output") and ONE GENERATED file (internal/cli/auth.go newAuthSetupCmd RunE body; header "// Generated by CLI Printing Press ... DO NOT EDIT.") — the auth.go edit is the reason this patch entry exists. The parallel prose surfaces (README.md auth narrative ~L123 + 401 troubleshooting ~L365; SKILL.md Auth Setup ~L149) are hand-editable and need no patch entry. Doc/hint correction only — no tool-surface, manifest, or dependency change; tools-manifest.json is unchanged and .manuscripts/<run>/research.json (auth_narrative/troubleshoot) is press-emitted and intentionally left lagging the corrected docs.
validated_outcome: go build/vet/test green under go1.26.5 (all internal packages pass). `auth setup` output smoke-tested: it now prints the 4-step browser sign-in flow (no Obsidian/plugin/app needed) and `--launch` appends the sign-in URL — the previous "No setup URL is configured for this CLI" dead-end is gone. Live dogfood --level quick PASS 16/16 (8 skipped) against the real Snipd API. `cli-printing-press publish validate` 11/11 PASS (manifest, transcendence, phase5, go mod tidy, govulncheck, go vet, go build, --help, --version, verify-skill, manuscripts). Re-grep confirms zero "pair the CLI" / "re-pair" / "from the Snipd app" phrasing remains outside .manuscripts/. Token never printed; package leak scan clean.
known_followups: Phase 2 (v1.1, after #1513 merges): the guided `auth login` command automates the browser sign-in + poll (like the Obsidian plugin's Connect button and `gh auth login`), then re-points these runtime hints and `auth setup` to lead with the one-command path, keeping the manual browser steps as the fallback. Spec: specs/auth-onboarding-v1.1.md.
